register.label: Farmland & farm viability
register.note: severed fields, tower/access disruption, and ag-economy ripple beyond the land itself

register.label: Scenic & recreation
register.note: viewshed and the Tuscarora Trail crossings, lost by the public, owned by no one

register.label: Groundwater & wells
register.note: the tower-foundation cost premium is now estimated (below); but karst aquifer / private-well contamination risk to residents stays unpriced

register.label: Ecosystems
register.note: wetlands, forest, wildlife connectivity the corridor crosses, non-market ecosystem services

register.label: Equity & community
register.note: a wealth transfer from local (mostly rural) residents to a regional grid / demand elsewhere; sense of place & option value

register.label: Health, stress & uncertainty
register.note: landowner stress through siting, perceived health effects, real, not monetized

assumptions: Easement take: taken acres × assessed land $/ac × 50–90% (easement, not fee-simple).

Diminution: distance-decay on market value, crossed/abutting 2–20%, fading to ~0 past ¼ mi (Brinkley & Leach ↔ recent HVTL metas), scaled by land type (residential full, business/industrial 0.3–0.6, ag 0.35–0.65).
Lost development potential: UDA/SWSA parcels the ROW crosses lose the development premium above ag value ($9,500/ac) on a band 2–4× the easement, at 20–60%. The most speculative component.

Tax loss: lost current taxable value × $0.48/$100 (confirmed, 2025 RE land book), PV 20 yr @ 4%. Enrolled RA land taxed at $370/ac use value (LOW) vs full FMV (HIGH); development value untaxed until built. Context: the county's total real-estate levy is $91M/yr.

Market adjustment: assessed values scaled ×1.06–1.28 to market, from Frederick's own 2024–26 sales (median assessment/sales ratio ~0.89 county-wide, ~0.85 for RA/rural). Applied to the private loss, not the county tax loss.

County utility tax (rough, forward-looking): the 765kV line is SCC-assessed public-service property, taxed by Frederick at $0.48/$100, est. ~$5M/mi assessed × 15–30 mi in-county (route TBD; no substation in Frederick VA). Net county = this gain − the RE loss.

Geotechnical hazard premium (project/ratepayer cost, not county/residents): ~5/mi towers, 20–40% in karst/expansive ground × $40k–$400k extra foundation each (285 ac carbonate + 208 ac shrink-swell of the 1,337-ac ROW; 3 sinkholes, 41 faults).
Assessed values are fair-market basis (median ~$17.4k/ac), so no use-value suppression in the loss itself.
